CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-13-2015 Continued To Oct-21-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-09-2015 Continued To Aug-26-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Ravi Srivatsav, Elasticbox, Inc.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Dec-11-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CHRISTOPHER J. ARCHIBALD OF THE ARCHIBALD & BERENJI, ALC, TEL. NO. (310) 855-3270 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JYAN FERNG OF THE SV EMPLOYMENT LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (650)265-0222 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS RAVI SRIVATSAW AND ELASTICBOX, INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings - The court grants the motion to compel arbitration and stay. The court decides the issue of arbitrability. Defendant fails to present clear and unmistakable evidence showing that the parties intended to submit that issue to the arbitrator. The reference to the JAMS rules is insufficient. See Ajamian v. CantoreCO2E, L.P. (2012) 203 Cal.App.4th 771. Plaintiff fails to demonstrate unconscionability. Both procedural and substantive unconscionability must be present. Plaintiff presents evidence demonstrating procedural unconscionability. Plaintiff does not establish substantive unconscionability. The arbitration agreement is bilateral, any fee award will comply with California law, including FEHA, and plaintiff is not obligated to pay arbitration fees that exceed court fees. The court will take a look on the PAGA Claims and will consider whether to sign an order to avoid filing of another motion infront of the court. Order signed after the hearing. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098, TEL. NO. (415)717-9768
